Business Structure Income Tax – An introduction Income Tax - An introduction

The purpose of this seminar is to provide you with an overview of the
current income tax laws applicable to the energy industry.  We will
discuss the tax treatment of interest, dividends, capital gains,
and other types of income.  We will also discuss the tax treatment of
foreign income and the foreign tax credit.  We will discuss the
tax treatment of partnerships and other pass-through entities.

The seminar will be held on Wednesday, November 15, 2000 from
9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. in EB 5C2.  Please RSVP to me at

K. Smith,

I am writing to you to let you know that I have been selected to participate in the Enron Research Group's (ERG) new project, "Energy Derivatives and the Internet of Things."  I am currently a Research Associate in the Energy Derivatives group at the University of Texas at Austin.  I am a graduate of the University of Texas at Austin with a B.S. in Mathematics and a Ph.D. in Finance.  I have worked in the energy derivatives industry for the past 10 years.  I have worked in the energy derivatives industry as a trader, analyst, and research analyst.  I have also worked in the financial derivatives industry as a consultant.  I have a strong background in mathematics and I am very excited about the opportunity to work on this project.

Subject: RE: FW: Enfolio Contract with CPS


Kim,

I am not sure if you have received this, but I wanted to let you know that
I have been working with the CPS attorney and have been able to get a
resolution to the issue.  I am still waiting for the final version of the
agreement, but I am told that it will be in the next couple of days.  I will let you
know as soon as I receive it.

Thanks,

KW

Enron's Dabhol Pwr To Be Sold To MSEB, Maharashtra State Electricity Board
2001-10-23 15:00 (New York)

     (Adds details of Dabhol Pwr sale in 11th paragraph.)

     New Delhi, Oct. 23 (Bloomberg) -- Enron Corp.'s Dabhol Power Co.
said it has reached an agreement with Maharashtra State Electricity
Board to sell the project to the state for $1.5 billion, or $1.75
per MMBtu of power.

     The sale is subject to regulatory approvals, including a
completion of a feasibility study, the company said in a statement.

     Dabhol, India's largest power project, is India's largest
foreign investment, with a $3.5 billion cost.

     The sale is expected to close in the third quarter of 2001, the company
said.

     Dabhol's power is sold to Maharashtra through a joint venture with
MSEB.

We are pleased to announce that we have appointed John Sherriff as our new Chief Executive Officer. John has been Managing Director of the Retail Business at the University of Birmingham for the last five years.  John has a wealth of experience in the retail industry and will be joining Enron in the UK.  John will report to me and will report to Jeff Skilling.

I would like to take this opportunity to thank John for his many contributions to Enron and wish him the very best of success in his new role.

I look forward to working with John in the coming months.

Sincerely,

Jeff

agreement to sell its stake in the Dabhol Power Co. (DPC) power project in

with them to complete the project," he added.
